project.name: musubi
project.description: Musubi is an open-source, self-hostable collaborative calendar where events are decoupled from calendars — linked into one or more shared calendars, edited once, and visible everywhere — with two-way external sync (Google, Apple/iCloud, CalDAV) and realtime updates. It is a pnpm + Turborepo monorepo pairing an Express/Drizzle/Postgres backend with an Expo/React Native client and shared TypeScript packages. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Local notification service that schedules, upserts, cancels, and reconciles per-event reminders (including recurring occurrences) via expo-notifications, tracking scheduled IDs in the SQLite notifications table.
nodes.languageNotes: Computes the next future occurrence for recurring events before scheduling and persists the OS notification identifier for later cancellation.

nodes.name: upsertEventNotification
nodes.summary: Cancels any existing reminder for an event, computes the next trigger, schedules a new OS notification, and records or updates its identifier in the cache.

nodes.name: reconcileEventNotifications
nodes.summary: Reconciles scheduled reminders against the current event set, cancelling stale notifications and re-upserting reminders for events that still opt in.

nodes.name: nextTrigger
nodes.summary: Computes the next future reminder timestamp for an event, expanding recurring events to find the upcoming occurrence.

nodes.name: registerForPushNotificationsAsync
nodes.summary: Sets up the Android notification channel and requests notification permissions from the OS.

nodes.name: useCalendarsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding the user's calendars and per-calendar visibility/selection, with actions to load, update, and remove calendars driven by the API layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Zustand create() store; no explicit function export detected by the parser since the store is an arrow-based factory.

nodes.name: useEventsStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store for the in-memory event list with local add/update/remove actions used to apply live SSE changes and cache-backed reloads.
nodes.languageNotes: Zustand store; local mutators mirror server pushes and keep the SQLite cache and notifications in sync.

nodes.name: _layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Expo Router layout that bootstraps the client: runs DB migrations, loads fonts, applies theme, enforces a minimum client version, handles incoming .ics deep links, and registers push notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Expo Router file-based routing conventions; nested AppLoader/AppContent components gate rendering on migrations, fonts, and session readiness.

nodes.name: handleIncomingUrl
nodes.summary: Detects incoming .ics deep-link URLs, fetches/reads their contents, parses them via parseICS, and stashes the result as a pending import in the import store.

nodes.name: AppContent
nodes.summary: Core app shell component that wires up session, routing guards, version-gating, splash hiding, and deep-link listeners before rendering the router stack.

nodes.name: AppLoader
nodes.summary: Applies the active color scheme and theme, sets the system background color, and gates rendering of AppContent until the server context is ready.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default exported root layout that registers push notifications and wraps the app in the ServerProvider and other top-level providers.

nodes.name: UpdateRequiredModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen blocking modal shown when the installed client version is below the server-required minimum, prompting the user to update via a store link.

nodes.name: UpdateRequiredModal
nodes.summary: Renders the update-required screen displaying current vs required version and an update button that opens the store URL.

nodes.name: url.ts
nodes.summary: Constants module defining the default server URL and the API version string used to build API request paths.

nodes.name: migrations.js
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Drizzle migrations bundle that imports the SQL migration files and journal metadata for the client's local SQLite database.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated by drizzle-kit; aggregates SQL migration text and journal into a single migrations export for expo-sqlite's useMigrations hook.

nodes.name: ics.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the ICS parser, verifying parseICS correctly extracts events from an iCalendar string.

nodes.name: ics.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight iCalendar (.ics) parser that unfolds lines, unescapes text, parses dates, and extracts VEVENT entries into structured event objects.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled RFC 5545 line-unfolding and property parsing without external dependencies.

nodes.name: parseDate
nodes.summary: Parses an iCalendar date/date-time value into a Date, handling date-only and UTC timestamp formats.

nodes.name: parseICS
nodes.summary: Parses a raw iCalendar string into an array of events, reading SUMMARY/DESCRIPTION/LOCATION and DTSTART/DTEND properties per VEVENT block.

nodes.name: useImportStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding a pending ICS import (parsed events) so a deep-linked calendar file can be picked up by the UI after navigation.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Package entry point that instantiates the Drizzle Postgres client (`db`) from config and re-exports the schema plus every query module for the @musubi/db package.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel that both re-exports query modules and constructs the shared drizzle-orm/node-postgres connection singleton.

nodes.name: calendars.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle query helpers for calendars: CRUD, invite-token resolution, membership management, and cascade cleanup that drops events left orphaned when a calendar is removed.

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle query helpers for events: creation with calendar/user linking, soft-delete via deletedAt, incremental fetch for a user since a timestamp, and purge of expired tombstones.

nodes.name: google.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle query helpers for Google account linkage: check calendar-scope authorization, list connected account IDs, fetch refresh tokens, and clear stored OAuth tokens on disconnect.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Central Drizzle schema defining all Postgres tables (users, sessions, accounts, calendars, events, memberships, invites, external/CalDAV sync, federation) and their relations for the entire application.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-file Drizzle pgTable schema with 27 exported tables/relations serving as the shared source of truth for the whole monorepo's data layer.

nodes.name: createCalendar
nodes.summary: Inserts a calendar (idempotent via onConflictDoNothing) and adds its owner as the first calendar member.

nodes.name: getCalendarIDFromToken
nodes.summary: Resolves an invite token to the calendar ID it grants access to via the calendarInvites table.

nodes.name: removeCalendar
nodes.summary: Deletes a calendar and cascades cleanup by dropping events that are no longer linked to any surviving calendar.

nodes.name: getUsersCalendars
nodes.summary: Returns all calendars a user is a member of via the calendarMembers relation.

nodes.name: getUserRoleForCalendar
nodes.summary: Looks up a user's role (owner/editor/viewer) for a specific calendar for permission checks.

nodes.name: addCalendarMember
nodes.summary: Adds a user to a calendar as a read-only viewer, ignoring conflicts on existing membership.

nodes.name: createEvent
nodes.summary: Inserts an event (defaulting its origin calendar), records the creator in eventUsers, and links the event to the given calendars via calendarEvents.

nodes.name: getUsersEvents
nodes.summary: Fetches events visible to a user by joining membership, calendar-event links, and events, optionally filtered incrementally by an updatedAt cutoff for delta sync.

nodes.name: removeEvent
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes an event by stamping deletedAt so offline clients can learn of the tombstone before it is purged.

nodes.name: googleCheck
nodes.summary: Checks whether a user has a linked Google account granting the Calendar OAuth scope.

nodes.name: getGoogleAccountIDs
nodes.summary: Lists a user's Google account IDs that hold the Calendar scope, filtering by granted scope.

nodes.name: getGoogleRefreshToken
nodes.summary: Retrieves the stored Google OAuth refresh token for a user's linked account.

nodes.name: cleanUsersGoogleTokens
nodes.summary: Clears stored Google OAuth tokens (refresh, access, expiry, scope) for a user on disconnect.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Email dispatch helper that configures a nodemailer transport and sends transactional emails (to/subject/html) for the API.

nodes.name: sendEmail
nodes.summary: Creates a nodemailer transport and sends an HTML email to the given recipient, logging success or failure.

nodes.name: password_reset.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the HTML body for the password reset email, interpolating the user name, reset URL, and expiry window into a styled template.

nodes.name: getPasswordResetHtml
nodes.summary: Returns the fully rendered HTML markup for a password reset email given the user name, reset URL, and expiry duration.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Central Better Auth configuration wiring the Drizzle adapter, email/password with reset flow, Google social sign-in, account linking, and a hook that provisions a personal calendar for every new user.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Better Auth's builder pattern with plugins (bearer, expo) and databaseHooks for post-registration side effects.

nodes.name: calendar.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Zod schema and inferred type for calendars, covering native and external-sync (Google/CalDAV) origins, membership, roles, and default-calendar flags. Also exports the providerFlavor helper that maps a calendar's sync origin to a display flavor (e.g. iCloud CalDAV to "apple").
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod z.infer to derive the TS Calendar type from the runtime schema, and CalendarSchema.extend to compose CalendarWithEventsSchema.

nodes.name: providerFlavor
nodes.summary: Resolves the display flavor for a calendar's sync origin, returning "apple" for CalDAV calendars pointed at iCloud and otherwise the raw provider (or null for native Musubi calendars).

nodes.name: event.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Zod schema and inferred type for calendar events, including timing, all-day/cancellation flags, recurrence, multi-calendar membership, and the origin calendar that governs edit rights.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses z.coerce.date() to accept serialized date strings and coerce them to Date on parse.

nodes.name: user.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Zod schema and inferred type for users, capturing id, name, validated email, and optional avatar image.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the Musubi API on node:22-alpine, using pnpm/corepack to install only the @musubi/api workspace subtree, then running drizzle-kit migrations before starting the server.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-stage build (base -> deps -> runner) leverages pnpm workspace filtering (--filter @musubi/api...) and layered COPY of package manifests to cache dependency installs separately from source.

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Base stage on node:22-alpine that enables corepack and pins pnpm 10.33.2, setting the /repo workdir shared by later stages.

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Dependency stage that copies workspace manifests (api, db, auth, types, config) and runs a frozen-lockfile pnpm install filtered to the @musubi/api subtree.

nodes.name: runner
nodes.summary: Runtime stage that copies application and shared package source, sets NODE_ENV=production, exposes the API port, and runs drizzle-kit migrate before launching the API server.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker image for the @musubi/docs Astro site: installs pnpm, builds the docs workspace, and serves the compiled server entry on port 4321.
nodes.languageNotes: Copies the full monorepo and uses pnpm workspace filtering (--filter @musubi/docs) to build only the docs package.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Runtime image stage built from node:lts that runs the compiled Astro SSR server (dist/server/entry.mjs) bound to 0.0.0.0:4321.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps node_modules, build artifacts, and local env files out of the image build sent to the daemon.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of runtime environment variables covering Postgres credentials, the API server port, Better Auth secrets, SMTP email, Google OAuth client IDs, CalDAV encryption key, and the external-sync polling interval.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the full runtime contract consumed by the API and docker-compose (DATABASE_URL, BETTER_AUTH_*, GOOGLE_* OAuth, CALDAV_ENC_KEY, EXTERNAL_SYNC_INTERVAL_MIN).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and entry-point documentation for Musubi, covering motivation, current features, sync providers, roadmap, how to try it, self-hosting instructions, architecture, and contributing.

nodes.name: docker-compose.selfhost.yml
nodes.summary: Generic self-hosting Compose stack that builds the API from apps/api/Dockerfile and publishes its port on the host, paired with an embedded Postgres 17 service gated by a health check.
nodes.languageNotes: Sibling of the Dokploy-oriented docker-compose.yml; differs by publishing ports directly instead of only exposing them behind an external reverse proxy.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Default Dokploy-targeted Compose stack that builds the API image, attaches it to an external dokploy-network for reverse-proxy routing, and runs a health-checked Postgres 17 database.
nodes.languageNotes: Only exposes the API port (no host publish) because Dokploy's reverse proxy fronts it via the external dokploy-network.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root workspace manifest for the Musubi monorepo, declaring the pnpm/Turborepo workspace, top-level scripts, packageManager pin, and shared devDependencies.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace configuration defining package globs, node linker mode, allowed build scripts, dependency overrides, and minimum-release-age exclusions.

nodes.name: turbo.json
nodes.summary: Turborepo pipeline configuration declaring the task graph (build, dev, and related tasks) and their dependency/cache behavior across workspace packages.

nodes.name: api
nodes.summary: Dokploy API service built from apps/api/Dockerfile, wired to the db service via DATABASE_URL and exposed behind the external reverse-proxy network.

nodes.name: db
nodes.summary: Postgres 17-alpine database service with a persistent data volume and a pg_isready health check that gates API startup.

nodes.name: api
nodes.summary: Self-hosting API service built from apps/api/Dockerfile that publishes its port directly on the host for use behind a user-supplied reverse proxy.

nodes.name: db
nodes.summary: Embedded Postgres 17-alpine database with a persistent data volume and pg_isready health check for the self-hosting stack.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything plugin configuration; sets the output language for generated knowledge-graph content to English.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Persisted per-file content fingerprints used by the Understand-Anything plugin for incremental change detection, so unchanged files are skipped on re-analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Large generated artifact (~9k lines) mapping file paths to hashes; not hand-edited.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: The committed Understand-Anything knowledge graph for Musubi — the serialized nodes and edges (files, functions, layers, relationships) browsed by the dashboard and chat tools.
nodes.languageNotes: Large generated artifact (~14.6k lines) representing the full codebase graph; committed so contributors browse locally without token cost.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Analysis run metadata for the Understand-Anything knowledge graph: last-analyzed timestamp, source git commit hash, schema version, and analyzed file count.

nodes.name: 0000_solid_captain_britain.sql

nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle-generated SQLite migration using backtick-quoted identifiers.

nodes.name: notifications_table
nodes.summary: Local table tracking scheduled event notifications keyed by identifier, eventID, and triggerDate.

nodes.name: 0001_next_stark_industries.sql
nodes.summary: Second Drizzle SQLite migration creating the events and sync_meta tables and recreating the notifications table for the client's offline event cache.
nodes.languageNotes: Recreates notifications via a __new_ shadow table, the standard Drizzle SQLite pattern for altering constraints.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Local cache of calendar events with creator, timing, recurrence, and calendar-membership columns for offline rendering.

nodes.name: sync_meta
nodes.summary: Key-value metadata table storing sync bookkeeping (e.g., last-sync markers) for the client's data pipeline.

nodes.name: 0002_loving_psynapse.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle SQLite migration adding an originCalendarID column to the events table to track which source calendar each cached event belongs to.

nodes.name: 0003_classy_vermin.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle SQLite migration adding a non-null offsetMinutes column (default 15) to notifications_table for configurable reminder lead time.

nodes.name: 0000_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM migration snapshot 0000 for the Expo client's local SQLite cache, defining the initial notifications_table schema.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated Drizzle snapshot capturing the SQLite dialect table/column/index definitions at migration checkpoint 0000.

nodes.name: 0001_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM migration snapshot 0001 adding the events and sync_meta tables alongside notifications_table for the client's offline SQLite store.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated Drizzle snapshot; prevId links it to the 0000 checkpoint to form the migration chain.

nodes.name: 0002_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM migration snapshot 0002 evolving the events, notifications_table, and sync_meta schema for the client's local SQLite cache.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated Drizzle snapshot chained from checkpoint 0001 via prevId.

nodes.name: 0003_snapshot.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle ORM migration snapshot 0003, the latest checkpoint of the events, notifications_table, and sync_meta schema for the client's offline SQLite store.

nodes.name: _journal.json
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration journal tracking the ordered list of applied migrations (0000 through 0003) for the Expo client's local SQLite database.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle's journal indexes each migration by tag and timestamp; it drives the ordering the migrator replays at runtime.

nodes.name: eas.json
nodes.summary: EAS build and submit configuration for the Expo client, defining development, preview, and production build profiles and store submission settings.
nodes.languageNotes: EAS build profiles typically layer development/preview/production settings for Expo Application Services.

nodes.name: package.json

nodes.name: tsconfig.json

nodes.name: 0000_square_dexter_bennett.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Drizzle migration establishing the core schema: Better Auth tables (user, session, account, verification) plus the calendar domain (calendars, events, members, invites, and join tables) with foreign keys and lookup indexes.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle-generated migration using statement-breakpoint markers; auth tables follow the Better Auth naming convention.

nodes.name: account
nodes.summary: Better Auth OAuth/credential accounts linking external providers to a user, storing access/refresh tokens, scopes, and optional password.

nodes.name: calendar_events
nodes.summary: Join table associating events with calendars (many-to-many event-to-calendar membership).

nodes.name: calendar_invites
nodes.summary: Shareable calendar invite tokens with expiry and maximum-use limits for joining a calendar.

nodes.name: calendar_members
nodes.summary: Membership join table linking users to calendars they can access.

nodes.name: calendars
nodes.summary: Core calendars table owned by a creator, with display name and color.

nodes.name: event_users
nodes.summary: Join table associating events with participating users.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Core events table with creator, name, color, and start/end timestamps; extended by later migrations.

nodes.name: session
nodes.summary: Better Auth session table tracking tokens, expiry, IP address, and user agent per user.

nodes.name: user
nodes.summary: Better Auth user table with name, email, verification flag, and avatar image.

nodes.name: verification
nodes.summary: Better Auth verification table holding time-limited identifier/value pairs for email or token verification.

nodes.name: 0001_funny_nekra.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the user_settings table for per-user preferences (kanji display, default calendar view, week start day).

nodes.name: user_settings
nodes.summary: Per-user preferences: show_kanji, default_calendar_view, and week_starts_on; extended by several later migrations.

nodes.name: 0002_flaky_natasha_romanoff.sql
nodes.summary: Migration enriching the events table with all-day flag, description, location, cancellation flag, organizer, recurrence rule, and URL columns.

nodes.name: 0003_medical_sebastian_shaw.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the user_status table tracking sponsor and premium flags per user.

nodes.name: user_status
nodes.summary: Per-user status flags (is_sponsor, is_premium) associated with a user_id.

nodes.name: 0004_legal_maestro.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a notifications_on_by_default boolean column to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0005_green_vin_gonzales.sql
nodes.summary: Migration dropping the default value on the events.id column (client-generated ids).

nodes.name: 0006_mushy_mac_gargan.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a time_locale column (default en-UK) to user_settings for time formatting.

nodes.name: 0007_windy_doorman.sql
nodes.summary: Migration introducing the first-generation Google Calendar sync tables (google_calendars, google_events); later dropped in favor of a generic external provider model.

nodes.name: google_calendars
nodes.summary: First-generation Google Calendar mapping table (calendar_id to google_calendar_id with sync_token); superseded by external_calendars.

nodes.name: google_events
nodes.summary: First-generation Google event mapping table (event_id to google_event_id); superseded by external_events.

nodes.name: 0008_broken_penance.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a user_id foreign key and uniqueness constraint to the google_calendars table.

nodes.name: 0009_neat_meteorite.sql
nodes.summary: Migration introducing the generic external provider sync model: external_calendars and external_events tables that abstract over Google, CalDAV, and other providers.

nodes.name: external_calendars
nodes.summary: Provider-agnostic mapping of a local calendar to an external calendar (provider, external_calendar_id, sync cursor).

nodes.name: external_events
nodes.summary: Provider-agnostic mapping of a local event to an external event (provider, external_event_id, etag for change detection).

nodes.name: 0010_high_bloodstorm.sql
nodes.summary: Migration dropping the legacy google_calendars and google_events tables now replaced by the generic external provider model.

nodes.name: 0011_boring_mentallo.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the caldav_accounts table storing CalDAV server credentials (encrypted password) per user.

nodes.name: caldav_accounts
nodes.summary: CalDAV account credentials per user: server_url, username, and encrypted_password.

nodes.name: 0012_late_anthem.sql
nodes.summary: Migration reworking uniqueness constraints for multi-account support: adds account_id to external_calendars and rekeys caldav_accounts and external_calendars uniqueness.

nodes.name: 0013_zippy_baron_zemo.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding an account_label column to external_calendars for display of the source account.

nodes.name: 0014_robust_sister_grimm.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a deleted_at timestamp to events for soft deletion / tombstoning.

nodes.name: 0015_deep_longshot.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a role column (default editor) to calendar_members introducing per-member permissions.

nodes.name: 0016_supreme_puppet_master.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding an origin_calendar_id foreign key to events (set null on delete) tracking the event's source calendar.

nodes.name: 0017_fine_brood.sql
nodes.summary: Migration changing calendar_members.role default to viewer and adding a theme column to user_settings.

nodes.name: 0018_cute_blue_shield.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding is_default to calendars and an onboarded flag to user_settings supporting the personal-default-calendar and onboarding features.

nodes.name: 0019_lean_mattie_franklin.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the user_avatars table storing avatar binary data and mime type keyed by user id.

nodes.name: 0020_opposite_ulik.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration that changes the default value of user_settings.default_calendar_view to 'month'.

nodes.name: 0021_lumpy_supreme_intelligence.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration adding a non-null calendar_id to external_events, backfilling it by joining calendar_events and external_calendars, then swapping the uniqueness constraint and resetting sync cursors for empty mirrors.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Postgres UPDATE...FROM with a JOIN to backfill, keeping the update-target only in the WHERE clause (Postgres restriction). Nulls stale sync cursors so the next sync re-imports fully.

nodes.name: 0022_long_demogoblin.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration adding a non-null calendar_order jsonb column (default empty array) to user_settings for persisting user calendar ordering.

nodes.summary: Drizzle migration replacing the time_locale column on user_settings with explicit time_format ('24h') and date_format ('dmy') columns.

nodes.summary: Drizzle migration creating the member_tokens table (hashed member tokens per user) with a supporting user index, used by the federation member-token flow.

nodes.name: member_tokens
nodes.summary: Table storing hashed member tokens keyed by user, with id, created_at, user_id and token_hash columns.

nodes.name: 0025_smiling_preak.sql
nodes.summary: Drizzle migration creating the musubi_accounts table storing per-user linked remote Musubi server accounts with encrypted tokens, backing Musubi-to-Musubi federation.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Musubi is: an open-source, self-hostable collaborative calendar built as a pnpm + Turborepo monorepo. It covers the motivation, the current feature set, supported sync providers, and how to run or self-host the project. Read this first to get the map before we walk the territory.

tour.title: The Knot: Events & Calendars
tour.description: This is the single most important concept in Musubi (its namesake, 結び / "knot"): an event is NOT owned by a calendar. The shared Zod event schema carries a multi-calendar membership plus an origin calendar that governs edit rights, while the calendar schema models both native calendars and external Google/CalDAV origins. Every layer downstream, schema, permissions, and sync, is shaped by this one decision.
tour.languageLesson: These packages define a Zod schema once and derive the TypeScript type from it with z.infer. One source of truth validates data at the wire boundary AND types it at compile time, so the client and server share an identical request/response contract.

tour.title: The Data Model, Explained
tour.description: Before reading raw SQL, this architecture doc narrates the whole data model: auth tables, calendars and events, the link tables that implement the knot, external-sync tables, and federation. It also explains cross-cutting decisions like soft-delete for delta sync and the custom bytea type. Building on Step 2's types, this shows how the concept lands in Postgres.

tour.title: The Central Schema
tour.description: The Drizzle schema is the server's source of truth for every Postgres table: users, sessions, calendars, events, memberships, invites, external/CalDAV sync, and federation. Note there is no calendarId column on events, membership lives entirely in the calendar_events junction table, exactly as the knot from Step 2 requires. This file is imported by every server query module.

tour.title: Migrations: The Knot in SQL
tour.description: The very first migration lays down the two tables that encode the knot: the events table (creator, name, timing, no calendar reference) and the calendar_events join table implementing many-to-many event-to-calendar membership. Later migrations extend events and add sync/federation tables incrementally. This is the physical realization of the design decision from Steps 2-4.
tour.languageLesson: Drizzle migrations are ordered, append-only SQL files (0000, 0001, ...). Each applies incremental changes so the schema evolves alongside the code; the generated schema.ts from Step 4 is the desired state, and the migration files are the diff history that gets there.

tour.title: Query Layer
tour.description: Handlers never touch SQL directly, they go through these Drizzle query helpers. The events queries handle creation with calendar/user linking, soft-delete via deletedAt (feeding delta sync), and incremental fetch since a timestamp; the calendar queries cover CRUD, invite-token resolution, membership, and cascade cleanup of orphaned events. This is where the knot's linking and unlinking logic actually lives.

tour.title: API Server Entry Point
tour.description: The Express entry point builds the app, wires CORS, auth, and middleware, mounts all versioned routes, and schedules background jobs (cleanup and near-realtime provider sync) before starting the server. This is the root of the server dependency graph, every handler and the sync engine hang off here. It defines the request rhythm the next steps unpack: validate, authorise, persist, sync, broadcast.

tour.title: Handlers & Permissions
tour.description: The event and calendar handlers implement the request rhythm: they validate input against the Step 2 schemas, call the permissions helpers to authorise, persist through the Step 6 queries, and push changes outward. Permissions is where the knot governs access, edit rights on an event derive from its origin calendar, not from whichever calendar you happen to be viewing it in.

tour.title: Realtime Broadcast & Auth
tour.description: The final beats of the request rhythm are broadcast and identity. The SSE stream handler pushes realtime add/update/remove notifications to a calendar's members after a change commits. Better Auth is the shared identity layer: Drizzle adapter, email/password with reset, Google sign-in, account linking, and a hook that provisions a personal calendar for every new user.

tour.title: External Sync Engine
tour.description: The generic sync engine selects a provider adapter, normalizes and upserts external changes, sweeps deletions, and pushes local edits back out. Crucially, external events are linked into calendars like any other event, the knot again. The Google and CalDAV adapters implement a common CalendarAdapter interface (OAuth/etag handling, recurrence normalization), and crypto encrypts stored provider secrets like CalDAV passwords.
tour.languageLesson: The adapter pattern lets one engine drive many providers: each adapter conforms to a shared interface, so adding a provider means writing an adapter, not touching the engine. This is the same normalization boundary that lets external events be linked in as first-class events.

tour.title: Musubi-to-Musubi Federation
tour.description: Federation lets one Musubi server share calendars with another. The federation handler accepts invites via member tokens and manages linked remote accounts; the schema backs this with member_tokens (hashed, per user) and musubi_accounts (remote server, user id, encrypted token). Conceptually a federated calendar is just another external origin linked in through the same machinery as Step 10.

tour.title: Client Boot & API Layer
tour.description: Now to the Expo client. The root layout bootstraps everything: runs local DB migrations, loads fonts and theme, enforces a minimum client version, handles incoming .ics deep links, and registers push notifications. The API service exposes a useApi hook wrapping Better Auth $fetch calls for every resource, and transparently routes federated calendars to the right remote server.

tour.title: Client State, Cache & Live Updates
tour.description: The client keeps events and calendars in Zustand stores for instant UI reads, backs them with an offline SQLite cache (via expo-sqlite + Drizzle) that tracks a last-sync watermark, and keeps everything fresh with an SSE stream hook that applies live add/update/remove changes from Step 9's broadcaster. This trio, store, cache, stream, is how the client stays responsive online and offline.

tour.title: Rendering & Recurrence
tour.description: The custom calendar renders a month grid and an interactive day timeline (hour lines, now indicator, drag-to-create, zoom). Behind the display, the shared recurrence engine caches parsed RRULEs and expands recurring events into concrete occurrences within a visible date range, editing a series means splitting or ending it, not touching every instance. This closes the loop from stored event to on-screen occurrence.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: Finally, how it ships. The multi-stage Dockerfile builds on node:22-alpine, uses pnpm to install only the @musubi/api workspace subtree, then runs drizzle-kit migrations before starting the server. The Compose stack builds that image, attaches it to a reverse-proxy network, and runs a health-checked Postgres 17, the same schema and migrations from Steps 4-5, now in production.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds separate the heavy build environment from the lean runtime image, and running migrations as a container start step means the database schema is always brought up to date before the server accepts traffic.
